title: Code with Claude Tokyo 2026: Opening Keynote
summary: Anthropic held a Code with Claude 2026 opening keynote in Tokyo featuring updates from engineering and product leaders. The event showcased the company's latest directions and announcements for developers.
writeup: This is the opening keynote of Anthropic's Code with Claude event in Tokyo, delivered hours after the surprise release of Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5. Caitlyn Les (head of engineering for the cloud platform) and Diane Penn (head of product for research) walk through the capability jump these fifth-generation models represent, the safeguard architecture that allowed them to ship broadly, and what is new on the Claude platform for developers building agents. The through-line is a concrete capability timeline from draft commit messages two years ago to autonomous overnight tasks, a 27-year-old OpenBSD vulnerability discovered by Claude, and now Fable 5's single-shot correctness and multi-day long-horizon autonomy.
key_points: Fable 5 leads SWE-bench Pro and its gap over competing models widens specifically on longer, more complex tasks, with early testers reporting single prompts producing work that previously took teams days or weeks.

Long-horizon autonomy is a first-class design goal: Fable 5 can run for days on a single goal, staying coherent across tasks spanning millions of tokens, and can dispatch and supervise sub-agents with greater reliability and cost-consciousness than prior models.

Fable 5 ships with a tiered safeguard system for high-risk domains: requests touching cybersecurity, biology, or chemistry are routed to Opus 4.8, clearly labeled, and billed at Opus prices, allowing broad availability while limiting misuse potential.

Mythos 5 is the same underlying model as Fable 5 but with the cyber and bio safeguards lifted; it is initially restricted to Project Glasswing partners, with life-sciences researchers set to gain access later in the month.
Year-over-year API volume on the Claude platform is up nearly 17 times, and Claude Code users now average 20 hours per week, indicating genuine workflow integration rather than experimental usage.

Two new Claude Managed Agents features ship at the event: scheduled deployments (agents run on a defined cadence) and environment variable vaults (agents can make authenticated API requests without direct key access).
